source_quote: There was a significant treatment main effect for systolic blood pressure (SBP; X2=5.22, df=1, p<.05)... Week 11 change: OROS-MPH 1.8 (SD 10.0, n=107), Placebo 0.1 (SD 8.3, n=109)
diastolic blood pressure (DBP; X2=12.13, df=1, p<.001)... Week 11 change: OROS-MPH 1.4 (SD 7.0, n=107), Placebo -0.8 (SD 6.2, n=109)
HR (X2=10.56, df=1, p<.01)... Week 11 change: OROS-MPH 2.2 (SD 10.2, n=107), Placebo 0.6 (SD 8.4, n=109)

Study uses 6-arm design (crossing TLFB x 3 intervention types) with discontinuous HLM models. No simple pairwise effect estimate (OR/RR/MD) is reported. Table 4 reports HLM coefficients: Basic BMI Intercept = -2.66 drinks/week vs TLFB/control (p<.05). Raw means Table 3 available for 6 groups across 4 timepoints but no standard two-arm comparison extractable.

Total DDQ (drinks per week) at follow-up: CPA 11.42 (SD 6.69, n=31), Control 14.54 (SD 11.93, n=70). Time x group interaction F(2,119)=3.59, p<.05. Both treatment groups reduced total drinks per week compared to control.
